commit.message:
8378952

Hi all,

  please review this fix to make G1 collector state correctly set clear_bitmap_in_progress() state for Concurrent Undo concurrent cycles.

If we do not do that, G1 does not correctly clear the bitmap parts that are used for evacuation failure handling, i.e. there may be leftovers from the concurrent start pause (we mark e.g. objects referenced by external roots there already).

I tried to do a failure analysis in the CR, in short there should be multiple asserts firing in this case, but overall G1 might not crash.

Note that we can't put an assert checking for this flag in `G1ConcurrentMark::cleanup_for_next_mark()`: due to GC breakpoints (the `AFTER CLEANUP STARTED` one) there might have been a full  gc just before calling that method that will clear the flag. In fact, there is an existing test that does exactly that. I can change the code to check and abort after the calls to `ConcurrentGCBreakPoints::at()`, but this seems unnecessary to me.

Testing: gha, asserts in prototype for JDK-8371720 are good now (it is also not possible to add that failing assert here because some surrounding code is not there)

Thanks,
  Thomas
